title: Meta unveils Muse Spark AI to rival Google, OpenAI; stock rallies 7%
snippet: Meta also released Contemplating mode, which runs multiple AI agents in parallel to boost reasoning power, allowing Muse Spark to take on the extended thinking modes of Google's Gemini Deep Think and OpenAI's GPT Pro.

title: How to use Google Gemini's new AI shopping features in India: Check step-by-step guide
snippet: Google has introduced new AI shopping features in India across the Gemini app, AI Mode in Search, and Circle to Search. Users can shop more efficiently with seamless transitions, product comparisons, and easy identification of items across platforms.

title: Keep your number hidden: WhatsApp reportedly begins rolling out username feature to change how you chat
snippet: WhatsApp is rolling out a new username feature that allows users to communicate without sharing phone numbers, according to a new report. Currently the feature is only available to a limited number of users

title: What is ‘Ghost Murmur’? The secret CIA heartbeat tracker used to find downed American pilot in Iran
snippet: A new report has revealed that the CIA used a secretive new technology called Ghost Murmur to locate the downed airman in IranThe technology works by detecting electromagnetic signals from heartbeats with quantum magnetometry

title: Mint Explainer | Anthropic biotech bet and AI's growing importance for Indian pharma
snippet: As foundational AI models muscle into biosciences and pharmaceuticals, what does it mean for India's $55-billion pharmaceutical industry? Mint explains.

title: Vivo T5 Pro 5G India launch date confirmed: Expected price, specifications and features
snippet: Vivo has confirmed that the Vivo T5 Pro 5G will be launching in India on 15th April at 12noon. The phone will be powered by Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 processor while featuring a massive 9,020 mAh battery.

title: X quietly launches Grok powered in-app photo editor that lets you edit images using only your words
snippet: Elon Musk's X platform has introduced an AI-powered photo editing feature using Grok AI. The feature is currently only available for iOS users while support for Android is expected soon.&nbsp;

title: Anthropic blocks the release of its most powerful AI model yet, launches new initative to tackle cyber threats
snippet: Anthropic's AI model Claude Mythos release has been put on hold by the company. The AI model is capable of discovering software vulnerabilities which have raised concerns for public safety and national security.

title: IT cos slash US teams; D2C luggage brands hit turbulence
snippet: Happy Thursday! Indian IT firms have begun cutting jobs in their US teams, according to filings. This and more in today's ETtech Morning Dispatch.

title: US-based data centre firm Equinix invests $95 million in Mumbai data centre, eyes further India expansion
snippet: With this, Equinix’s India investment has touched a total of $365 million. Per Cyrus Adaggra, president for Asia-Pacific at Equinix, APAC is being seen as a safer and more reliable investment destination.

title: AI drives Indian IT companies to cut US jobs
snippet: Indian IT firms are increasing job cuts in the US, with layoffs expected to accelerate due to AI's growing influence and slowing deal-making. title: TR Capital to deploy $1 billion in India secondaries; appoints Umang Agarwal as MD
snippet: TR Capital will deploy $1 billion in India over the next five years, across consumer, financial services, and healthcare, while selectively evaluating opportunities at the intersection of software and artificial intelligence (AI), managing partner Frederic Azemard told ET today.

title: Nine companies clear IndiaAI GPU tender-4 tech stage amid costs woes
snippet: According to Government e-Marketplace (GeM) tender status data, the qualified bidders include Paradigmit Technology Services, Tata Communications, RackBank Datacenters, Netmagic IT Services, E2E Networks, Yotta Data Services, Cyfuture India, Sify Digital Services and UrsaCompute.
